So....Burnham has made MP, as forecast. The Golden Boy is over the first artificial hurdle; on to the next.
Assuming he gets to No. 10, where the buck stops, what then?....he's no Messiah, wafting around making everything better. He will be a Freshman, a really new kid on the block, feeling his way, learning the ropes.He will inherit ALL the problems Prime Minister Starmer has got, national and world wide...his magic wand won't help him there......how will he do?...that is unknown, guesswork, supposition. In short, dangerous uncertainty...that's what his supporters have voted him into; the political cauldron, Pres.Truman's 'the heat of the kitchen,' from which there is no escape for four long years. Actually folks; I wish him well for the sake of the United Kingdom.

Real tourist places of india where tourist should visit rather GOLDEN triangle and Delhi, Kolkata 

North India

Ladakh

1. Tso Moriri Lake – A lesser-known but equally mesmerizing high-altitude lake compared to Pangong.


2. Hanle – Home to the world’s highest astronomical observatory, with surreal night skies perfect for stargazing.


3. Zanskar Valley – A dreamland for adventure lovers, famous for the frozen-river Chadar Trek and ancient monasteries.


4. Lamayuru – Known as the “Moonland of Ladakh” because of its other-worldly landscapes.


5. Umling La Pass – The highest motorable road in the world; pure thrill for bikers.


6. Turtuk – A peaceful border-side Balti village surrounded by apricot orchards and warm local culture.




---

Himachal Pradesh

1. Spiti Valley – A high-altitude cold desert with Tibetan-influenced villages, monasteries, and breathtaking views.


2. Tirthan Valley – Serene and lush, with rivers and forests—an offbeat alternative to crowded Manali.


3. Chitkul – The last inhabited village near the Indo-Tibetan border, with postcard-perfect wooden homes and mountains.


4. Jibhi & Jalori Pass – Hidden gems offering riverside stays and quiet treks amid pine forests.




 West India

Maharashtra

1. Ellora Caves (CH. Sambhaji Nagar) – UNESCO-listed rock-cut temples and monasteries carved into cliffs, showcasing ancient Indian art and architecture.


2. Ajanta Caves – Nearby Buddhist cave paintings dating back over 2,000 years.


3. Bhandardara – Calm lakes, waterfalls, and mountain views; ideal monsoon retreat.


4. Kaas Plateau (Satara) – Maharashtra’s “Valley of Flowers,” covered with wild blooms between August–October.


5. Lonar Crater Lake – Formed by a meteorite impact 50,000-60,000 years ago  a rare geological wonder. Only 3 In world 




🌿 South India

Kerala

1. Munnar – Rolling tea-covered hills, misty mornings, and British-era bungalows; one of India’s most scenic hill stations.


2. Varkala – Dramatic cliffs overlooking the Arabian Sea, known for its laid-back vibe and stunning sunsets.


3. Wayanad – Lush forests, waterfalls, and spice plantations perfect for trekking and eco-stays.


4. Athirapally Falls – Kerala’s biggest waterfall, often called the “Niagara of India.”


5. Kumarakom & Vembanad Lake – Beautiful backwaters surrounded by coconut palms and houseboats.




🌊 Islands

Lakshadweep Islands
 
India’s most pristine tropical paradise with turquoise lagoons, coral reefs, and white-sand beaches.
Ideal for scuba diving, snorkeling, and peaceful island life — far less commercial than the Maldives Islands even better in natural beauty

Dear Sir,

Amid the ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict disrupting oil supplies and pushing global crude above $110/barrel, India’s petrol prices have surged to ₹98–110 per litre. This volatility drains foreign exchange, fuels inflation, and enriches foreign suppliers and big corporates.
Pineapple Bioethanol offers an immediate, self-reliant alternative:
Production Cost: ₹40/litre (₹30 farmer support price + ₹10 processing)  
With 28% GST: ₹51.20/litre  
Recommended Selling Price: ₹60/litre (₹8.80 profit stays local)

Pilot Projects: Start with small-scale ethanol plants in Kerala’s pineapple-growing regions (e.g., Ernakulam, Thrissur) to test feasibility and optimize processes.
Policy Support: Advocate for lower GST rates (5–12%) and subsidies for biorefineries to reduce costs and attract investment.
Kudumbasree Integration: Train Kudumbasree units for waste collection and preprocessing, creating employment and ensuring supply chain efficiency.
By-Product Development: Market biogas, fertilizers, and biodegradable ropes to diversify revenue and reduce reliance on ethanol sales alone.
Public-Private Partnerships: Collaborate with private firms to fund biorefineries, leveraging Kerala’s agricultural expertise and global demand for biofuels.
Research and Development: Invest in R&D for cost-effective pretreatment methods and high-yield pineapple varieties (non-GM initially to avoid regulatory delays).


Key Advantages
Kerala alone can generate 13–27 million litres annually from pineapple waste, guaranteeing farmer income, creating jobs through Kudumbasree networks, and reducing import dependence. Unlike fossil fuels, every rupee invested circulates within the local economy, boosting buying power of common citizens.
Conclusion
In an era of geopolitical fuel shocks, pineapple bioethanol delivers cheaper, cleaner, and truly Atmanirbhar energy. Scaling this model will strengthen farmers, shield consumers from global crises, and build long-term economic resilience for Kerala and India. Immediate policy support for local processing units is strongly recommended.

The Tories left the country in an ECONOMIC mess after 14 years and the media want to make a comparison of progress after 24 months. Not to mention the current AMERICAN fiasco, wreaking World economies in its illegal war in IRAN, and MORE importantly, the RUSSIAN WAR in UKRAINEe. Countries have budgets and local MPs must recognise, that International issues are having the greater impact. I have been a Labour Party member since 1970, and I have had the confidence, that Labour is the best party with the right ETHOS for improving working class quality of life. Burnham is an MP, I did not vote for him in the Leadership challenge after Jeremy Corbyn stepped down, I voted for 3 ladies, (one of whom, is still in the cabinet and one not yet become a member of the cabinet) However, that was not be, and we have Sir Keir Starmer as our Prime Minister. If there is a leadership challenge, I expect the whole labour membership to back the current PM, because we must not let MEDIA B/S divert us from our election 2024 mandate. Which is a far bigger mountain to climb, than Andy Burnham's pimple in Makerfield. His DEVISIVE comments are damaging the Labour Party and our main obligation is to the 2024 mandate, and the 411 constituencies who voted for Labour. Not just a constituency in Greater Manchester, where their previous choice turned out to be a potential agitating infiltrator.

भारतीय मीडिया (गोदी मीडिया क्यों?): मुख्यधारा का एक बड़ा हिस्सा सत्ता से कड़े सवाल पूछने के बजाय "चाटुकारिता और मक्खन लगाने वाले" सवाल पूछता है। इनका फोकस जनता की समस्याओं (जैसे बेरोजगारी, महंगाई) पर नहीं, बल्कि सरकार को खुश करने और विपक्ष को घेरने पर होता है। इसी वजह से यह लोकतंत्र के फोर्थ पिलर (चौथे स्तंभ) के रूप में फेल साबित होती है और इसे 'गोदी मीडिया' कहा जाता है।

​अमेरिकन मीडिया (फोर्थ पिलर क्यों?): वहां के पत्रकार नेताओं की विचारधारा या उनकी ताकत की परवाह किए बिना "टफ और असहज करने वाले क्वेश्चन" पूछते हैं। उनका नजरिया 'अग्रैसिव' होता है, जिससे नेता जनता के प्रति जवाबदेह बनते हैं। यही तीखापन उनके लोकतंत्र के चौथे स्तंभ को मजबूती देता है।
